title: Person seriously injured in fall at Firefighting Academy facility
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A person was seriously injured in a fall at the Firefighting Academy in Stow on Tuesday morning.
Emergency crews responded at 9:05 a.m., providing care and requesting additional ambulances due to the severity of injuries.
Transportation by medical helicopter was unavailable due to weather; the person was taken to UMass Memorial Medical Center by ground.

title: TravelMag went on a seafood road trip in Maine and named these ‘can’t-miss’ restaurants. Which is your favorite?
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: TravelMag highlights The Clam Shack in Kennebunkport for its legendary fried clams in a Maine seafood road trip.
The tour includes stops like Eventide Oyster Co. in Portland and Five Islands Lobster Co. in Georgetown.
Bagaduce Lunch in Penobscot is praised as a hidden gem on TravelMag's list of must-visit seafood spots.

title: Ex-Stoughton cop Matthew Farwell’s DNA was on strap used to strangle Sandra Birchmore, prosecutors say
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: DNA evidence links former Stoughton officer Matthew Farwell to the strap used to strangle Sandra Birchmore in 2021.
Farwell believed Birchmore, found dead while pregnant, was carrying his child, although DNA excluded him as the father.
Prosecutors allege Farwell started exploiting Birchmore sexually when she was underage and maintained a secretive relationship until her death.

title: A young mother rescued her 2-year-old son after their car crashed into a Vermont river
author: John Waller
bullets: A 26-year-old mother rescued her 2-year-old son from a submerged car in the Ottauquechee River, Vermont.
Hartford firefighters arrived swiftly, and both mother and child were safe onshore by the time an ambulance arrived.
The mother managed to unbuckle her son, cut through an airbag, and swim them to safety; no serious injuries were reported.

title: All the places you can go on the Amtrak Downeaster
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: The Amtrak Downeaster runs 143 miles from Boston's North Station to Brunswick, Maine.
The train serves 12 stations in Massachusetts, New Hampshire, and Maine with five daily round-trips year-round.
One-way fares range from $3 to $38; seniors get 50% off, and pets and bikes are allowed with fees.

title: ‘Boston College is about to have a renaissance in basketball’: Dan Hurley’s take on Luke Murray
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Luke Murray, UConn assistant coach, hired to lead Boston College basketball following Earl Grant's firing.
Dan Hurley, UConn head coach, praised Murray's abilities and predicted a basketball renaissance for Boston College.
Luke Murray, son of actor Bill Murray, has been part of two national championships with UConn since 2021.

title: MBTA sets $80 round-trip train fare for World Cup games
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: MBTA sets an $80 round-trip fare for South Station to Gillette Stadium train rides during 2026 World Cup matches.
Tickets for the matches will be sold exclusively via the mTicket app starting April 8 at 11 a.m.
Special commuter rail service includes 14 express trains on match days and extended service on subway lines.

title: New on Netflix and more: Top streaming picks for April 2026
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Netflix releases new zombie thriller '28 Years Later: The Bone Temple' featuring Ralph Fiennes and Erin Kellyman.
Mel Brooks' classic 'Spaceballs' streams on Prime Video ahead of its sequel release next year.
'The Devil Wears Prada' streams on HBO Max and Hulu with a new legacyquel arriving in theaters soon.

title: Details emerge on fatal police shooting of 27-year-old woman in N.H.
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Megan Whiting, 27, died from multiple gunshot wounds during a police response to a domestic disturbance in Northfield, N.H.
Police say Whiting was armed with a knife near an injured resident when an officer fired; her death was ruled a homicide.
Authorities have not released the name of the officer involved or condition of the injured resident; bodycam footage remains unreleased.

title: Signature Healthcare in Brockton hit by cybersecurity incident
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Signature Healthcare in Brockton is managing a cybersecurity incident, affecting parts of their systems since Monday.
Ambulance redirection and canceled chemotherapy services are in effect while patient care continues under adapted procedures.
Retail pharmacies at 110 Liberty St. and 1 Donalds Way are temporarily closed due to the incident.
